steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test seq1_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: Test exits with code 0; stdout confirms lock created in DB, SR0 computed as SHA3-256("QS_LOCK_V1" || chain_id || asset || amount || dest_addr || expiry || nonce || pk_dilithium), and L1 lockWithSR0 transaction submitted without error

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: All Playwright test cases pass (exit code 0); JSON reporter output contains zero failed tests; UI lock creation flow reaches success state and a corresponding DB row is confirmed

steps.description: Verify lock row was created during this run window and pk_dilithium populated
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
steps.expected: Exactly one row is returned; status is 'locked' (terminal confirmed state) or 'pending' (awaiting L1 confirmation); pk_dilithium length is exactly 1952 bytes

steps.description: Verify totalLocked increased on Sepolia Vault
steps.expected: Returned uint256 value is strictly greater than 0 wei, confirming the Vault contract at 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 has received and recorded the locked asset amount

acceptance_criteria: Backend integration test (seq1_lock) exits with code 0 and stdout confirms SR0 computed and L1 lockWithSR0 submitted
Playwright lock.integration.spec.ts reports zero failed tests and the UI lock flow reaches success state
A locks row exists created within the last 5 minutes with status equal to 'locked' and pk_dilithium length equal to exactly 1952 bytes
Sepolia Vault totalLocked() returns a value strictly greater than 0 wei
No silent fallback or hardcoded address appears anywhere in the execution trace

issues.title: Unused variable with unwrap_or_default
issues.detail: Multiple instances of `let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()` where body is never used. Pattern appears 6 times (lines 671, 1217, 1235, 1479, 1499, 1517). While unwrap_or_default is not critical here (returns empty Value on deserialization failure), it masks potential deserialization errors silently.

issues.title: RPC Connection Timeout
issues.detail: The L1 RPC endpoint (rpc.sepolia.org) did not respond within the connection timeout window. This is an infrastructure issue, not a contract or code regression. Cloudflare returned HTTP 522 indicating the origin server was unreachable.

quality_findings.title: Frontend ML-DSA signature test suite completely dead — real signature verification bypassed in E2E
quality_findings.detail: The frontend E2E integration test for the Consumer Lock sequence fails before a single test runs due to `'@noble/post-quantum/ml-dsa'` not being exported by the installed package version. This means the ML-DSA signature that should be constructed and verified as part of the lock flow has NEVER been exercised by CI in this run. The backend passed and the DB row exists with `pk_dilithium` length 1952 — but the cross-layer contract that the frontend actually generates a valid ML-DSA-65 signature over the correct payload, submits it to the API, and the API verifies it, is untested. This is a direct path to silent signature-bypass: if the API's signature verification has a latent bug (e.g. skip_signature_verification env override, or the verification stub pattern from high-priority item #8), it would never be caught here. The 'real ML-DSA' fix referenced in the merge commit subject (`fix(e2e): close 3 follow-on findings … real ML-DSA`) is the exact feature that is now broken.
evidence: Error: Package subpath './ml-dsa' is not defined by "exports" in /home/runner/work/quantum-shield/quantum-shield/src/frontend/web/node_modules/@noble/post-quantum/package.json
at Object.<anonymous> (e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts:28:1) — import { ml_dsa65 } from '@noble/post-quantum/ml-dsa'
stats: expected=0, unexpected=0 — zero tests ran, exit_code=1

DB layer shows lock row created with pk_dilithium length=1952 and status=pending — backend accepted the request, but the frontend test that was supposed to produce and validate the real ML-DSA signature never executed
Merge commit subject: 'fix(e2e): close 3 follow-on findings (DB window + real ML-DSA + stranded-pending)' — the ML-DSA fix regressed in the same PR

quality_findings.title: unwrap_or_default() on resp.json() silently masks API response deserialization failures in E2E tests
quality_findings.detail: Six locations in the E2E test suite use `let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()` where the variable `body` is then never read. This matches high-priority silent-failure pattern #3 (`unwrap_or_default()` hiding parse errors). If the API returns a malformed JSON body (e.g. due to an internal error that still produces HTTP 200), the test will silently succeed with an empty `Value::Null` body and assert nothing. In the Consumer Lock sequence this is particularly dangerous: step completions, lock IDs, and status fields returned from the API are never validated against the actual response body in these code paths. The compiler itself flagged all six instances as unused variables.
evidence: sequence_e2e_test.rs:671: `let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default();` — compiler warning: unused variable `body`
Same pattern repeated at lines 1217, 1235, 1479, 1499, 1517 — 6 total occurrences
Compiler: `warning: unused variable: body` (6 warnings, all same pattern)
Backend test still passes (exit_code=0) despite none of these response bodies being asserted

quality_findings.title: L1 vault state unverifiable — DB shows pending lock but on-chain totalLocked() confirmation is missing
quality_findings.detail: The DB layer confirms a lock row exists with status='pending' and pk_dilithium=1952 bytes, but the L1 layer could not execute `totalLocked()` on the correct vault address (0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260) due to RPC timeout. The cross-layer invariant for Consumer Lock requires: DB row created → L1 vault balance increased → status transitions to 'locked'. With L1 unreachable, the full sequence cannot be validated and the lock may be permanently stranded in 'pending'. This is not merely an infra flap — the merge commit explicitly references fixing 'stranded-pending' as one of three regressions, and that fix cannot be verified here. If the background confirmation task swallows errors (`Ok(())` pattern, high-priority item #5), stranded-pending locks would accumulate silently.
evidence: DB output: `0x8dd964bde1712da94a31c51c4b7d30ba63bae4b02f17f1b1b7a51f3875e6accb|pending|1952` — status is pending, not locked
L1 stderr: `Error: HTTP error 522 with body: ... Connection timed out` against rpc.sepolia.org
cast call 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 totalLocked() returned no stdout — vault state unknown
Merge commit: 'fix(e2e): close 3 follow-on findings (DB window + real ML-DSA + stranded-pending)' — stranded-pending was a known regression; cannot confirm it is fixed this run

quality_findings.title: @noble/post-quantum package version missing ml-dsa export — wrong package version pinned
quality_findings.detail: The installed version of `@noble/post-quantum` in the frontend does not export the `./ml-dsa` subpath. The `@noble/post-quantum` package only added named subpath exports (including `ml-dsa`) in version 0.2.0+. The currently installed version appears to predate this. Since the merge commit claims to have introduced 'real ML-DSA' support, the package was either not upgraded, or was upgraded but `package-lock.json` / the lockfile was not committed correctly. This is a dependency pinning bug, not a transient failure.
evidence: Error: Package subpath './ml-dsa' is not defined by "exports" in node_modules/@noble/post-quantum/package.json
import { ml_dsa65 } from '@noble/post-quantum/ml-dsa' — the import was written for the newer API
The error is deterministic (not a network/timing issue) — it will reproduce on every CI run until the package version is corrected

fixer_proposals.title: Fix @noble/post-quantum package version to expose ml-dsa subpath export
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses findings from bug-hunter (critical): 'Module import error prevents test execution', 'Frontend ML-DSA signature test suite completely dead', and (medium): '@noble/post-quantum package version missing ml-dsa export'. The ./ml-dsa subpath export was introduced in @noble/post-quantum v0.2.0. The currently pinned version predates this. Upgrading package.json and removing the stale lockfile entry for @noble/post-quantum is the minimum fix to unblock the E2E spec from loading.

fixer_proposals.title: Replace silent unwrap_or_default() on response bodies with explicit error propagation in E2E tests
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses bug-hunter findings (high, low): 'unwrap_or_default() on resp.json() silently masks API response deserialization failures' and 'Unused variable with unwrap_or_default'. Six occurrences at lines 671, 1217, 1235, 1479, 1499, 1517 of sequence_e2e_test.rs use unwrap_or_default() and never read the result. Per fix rules, unwrap_or_default() is forbidden — these must propagate errors. Each call site is changed to .expect() with a contextual message (appropriate in test code where panic-on-failure is the correct behaviour, making the failure visible rather than silent).

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — verify stranded-pending lock confirmation task does not swallow errors
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses bug-hunter finding (high): 'L1 vault state unverifiable — DB shows pending lock but on-chain totalLocked() confirmation is missing'. The lock row is stuck in status='pending' and the L1 RPC is unreachable. The merge commit claimed to fix 'stranded-pending' but that fix cannot be verified. The concern is that the background confirmation task may be calling Ok(()) on RPC errors rather than propagating them, which would silently strand locks forever. The L1 RPC timeout is an infrastructure issue (HTTP 522) and cannot be fixed in code, but the error-swallowing pattern must be audited. No automatic patch is safe to produce without inspecting the actual confirmation task implementation — a wrong patch here could break confirmed locks or cause panic loops.

final_verdict.summary: The Consumer Lock sequence has two concrete failures: (1) the frontend Playwright spec crashes at import time because the installed @noble/post-quantum package does not export the './ml-dsa' subpath — zero tests ran, the ML-DSA signature path is entirely unverified by CI; (2) the L1 layer could not call totalLocked() due to an HTTP 522 RPC timeout on rpc.sepolia.org, which is a transient infrastructure outage (override rule 2 applied: downgrading this from a blocking failure to a retriable infra issue). The backend passed cleanly (4/4 tests, SR0 computed, exit 0), and the DB confirms a lock row with status='pending' and pk_dilithium=1952 bytes within the 5-minute window. The frontend package version bug is deterministic and will reproduce every run until corrected; the L1 failure should be retried with a healthy RPC endpoint.
must_fix_before_merge: Upgrade or pin @noble/post-quantum to a version that exports './ml-dsa' subpath (≥0.2.0) and commit the updated lockfile — frontend suite is completely dead without this
Verify and fix ML-DSA-65 signature generation and submission path in the frontend lock flow, which has never been exercised by CI in this run

Replace all six `let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()` dead-variable patterns in sequence_e2e_test.rs with actual assertions on the response body to eliminate silent API failure masking
Retry L1 totalLocked() verification against a healthy Sepolia RPC endpoint to confirm vault balance increased and stranded-pending fix is effective
Confirm DB lock status transitions from 'pending' to 'locked' after L1 confirmation — stranded-pending fix from the merge commit cannot be validated until L1 RPC is reachable
unresolved_questions: What exact version of @noble/post-quantum is currently pinned in package.json and package-lock.json, and why was it not updated when the ml-dsa import was introduced?
Is the stranded-pending background confirmation task actually fixed, or does it swallow errors silently (Ok(()) pattern)? Cannot confirm from this run because L1 RPC was unreachable.

Does the API have a skip_signature_verification environment variable or stub path that could accept unsigned/incorrectly-signed lock requests — the frontend signature path has never been validated by CI?
